parsed_review.recommendation: Approve and close CP00-133. The Risk A terminal review question boundary pack is a metadata-only, no-write, synthetic-only addition that satisfies every stated scope constraint; no changes required.
parsed_review.rationale: Scope and coverage verified against the staged diff. CP133_UNITS enumerates exactly the 10 planned units RP02.P09.M05.S18-RP02.P09.M06.S07 (first=RP02.P09.M05.S18, last=RP02.P09.M06.S07) covering next RP dependency, documentation update, command rerun, architecture review questions, security review questions, permission bypass questions, audit completeness questions, missing test questions, UI leak questions, and downstream readiness questions; phase split is M05=3/M06=7 and deliverable split is implementation=4/claude_review=2/security_audit=2/test=1/ui=1, matching the manifest, matrix counts (terminal_handoff=3, review=3, security_audit=2, test=1, ui_leak=1), validatePermissionKernelCp133Coverage assertions, and the contract validation script. Inheritance from CP132 is wired via imports and asserted (inherited result count 150, handoff-to-CP133, permission-bypass no-execution, command-rerun reference-only). Behavior posture is correct: terminal_handoff rows set reference_only=true with documentation_published=false, command_rerun_executed=false, commits_pack=false, marks_production_ready=false; review_questions keep executes_claude_review=false and grants_approval=false; security_audit_questions keep executes_permission_bypass=false, emits_audit_event=false, mutates_permission_policy=false; test_questions keep executes_tests=false; ui_leak_questions keep renders_ui=false; and downstream readiness grants_approval=false. freezeNoWriteResult and CP133_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION drive every result and the contract block to false across permission mutation, audit/product/database writes, idempotency persistence, locks, retry/rollback/compensation, export/external-share, AI/analytics, claude execution, hermes runtime, and LDIP; hidden_source_fields are listed without exposing counts/values (hidden_field_names_exposed=false, unauthorized_count_exposed=false). No runtime UI/API routes are added (runtime_ui_route_added/runtime_api_route_added=false), command and replay strings are inert anchors, HRX remains embedded (no split), and handoff is fixed to CP00-134 / RP02.P09.M06.S08 consistently across contract, README, source, and validation. Public exports, hidden source fields, and no_write_attestation are mirrored between source contract and JSON contract and enforced by the validation script. Unknown-case path fails closed (blocked_before_permission_evaluation, no writes). All Object.freeze usage preserves immutability. Cross-module reliance on CP132 field paths is guarded by validation/tests and matches the established pack pattern; nothing in the diff violates the boundary.
